items.text:
Subject: Summer Internship Application Deadline — Action Needed

Dear Marcus,

This is a reminder that applications for the Summer Internship Program are due Friday, March 14, by 5:00 PM. We've noticed that your profile in the Career Portal is still incomplete, so please log in and finish it before the deadline.

To submit a complete application, you must upload a current resume, a one-page cover letter, and two faculty references. All materials should be uploaded through the portal, not emailed. Applications that arrive after the deadline will not be reviewed.

Please also note that the interview-skills workshop on March 10 carries a $15 materials fee, payable at the Career Services front desk in Harmon Hall, Room 120. Students who attend the workshop receive priority scheduling for mock interviews.

If you have questions, stop by our office between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM, Monday through Friday, or call us at the number below.

Best regards,
Ms. Ortega
Career Services Advisor

format_metadata.subject: Summer Internship Application Deadline — Action Needed

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Marcus upload for a complete application?

options.A: A resume, a cover letter, and a workshop receipt
options.B: A resume, a portfolio, and two faculty references
options.C: A resume, a cover letter, and two faculty references
options.D: A cover letter, a portfolio, and a photo ID

questions.explanation: The email states that a complete application requires a current resume, a one-page cover letter, and two faculty references, all uploaded through the portal.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who attend the March 10 workshop?

options.A: They are exempt from the $15 materials fee.
options.B: They must submit their applications a day early.
options.C: They will be interviewed by faculty references.
options.D: They gain an advantage in scheduling mock interviews.

questions.explanation: The email says attendees receive priority scheduling for mock interviews, which implies they gain an advantage over students who do not attend.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce a newly created summer internship program
options.B: To remind a student to finish an application by the deadline
options.C: To collect payment for the interview-skills workshop
options.D: To confirm a scheduled mock interview appointment

questions.explanation: The email opens as a reminder about the application deadline and urges the student to complete an incomplete profile before Friday, March 14.

items.text:
Notice: Annual Locker Clean-Out and Renewal

The Facilities Office announces that all student lockers in the Weller Athletic Center must be emptied and renewed by Wednesday, May 20. Lockers that are not renewed by this date will be cleared, and the contents will be donated or discarded.

To renew a locker for the next semester, students should complete the following steps:
- Log in to the Facilities Portal and select "Locker Renewal."
- Pay the annual $30 locker fee online or at the front desk.
- Attach a new lock; old locks will be removed by staff on May 21.

Renewal is available from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday through Saturday. Personal items left in unrenewed lockers can be reclaimed at the Lost and Found in Room 108 until May 27. After that date, all unclaimed belongings will be given to local charities.

Questions may be directed to the Facilities Office at facilities@campus.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to lockers that are not renewed by May 20?

options.A: They will be assigned to new students.
options.B: They will be emptied and the contents removed.
options.C: They will be renewed at no extra charge.
options.D: They will be closed for the summer break.

questions.explanation: The notice states that lockers not renewed by May 20 will be cleared, with the contents donated or discarded.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who keep their old locks after May 20?

options.A: They will receive a refund of the $30 fee.
options.B: They will be given priority for renewal.
options.C: They will find their locks removed by staff.
options.D: They will keep the same locker for free.

questions.explanation: The notice says old locks will be removed by staff on May 21, so students who leave old locks on will find them taken off.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a new athletic center opening
options.B: To instruct students to renew or empty their lockers
options.C: To collect donations for local charities
options.D: To advertise a discount on locker fees

questions.explanation: The notice's central aim is to direct students to renew their lockers by the deadline or have them emptied, explaining the required steps and consequences.
